message:
style(fnp-python): cargo fmt cumsum + ptp conformance tests

`cargo fmt` reflowed two files in the `fnp-python` conformance suite:

- `crates/fnp-python/tests/conformance_cumsum.rs`
- `crates/fnp-python/tests/conformance_ptp.rs`

The diff is purely whitespace: short `format!(...)` invocations that
had been broken across multiple lines now fit on a single line under
the project's rustfmt width, and the `arrays_close` helper's chained
`a.iter().zip(b.iter()).all(...)` is wrapped onto three lines per the
configured chain-length heuristic. No identifiers, expressions, or
test cases changed; behavior is byte-identical at runtime.

These were left dirty by a previous `cargo fmt --all` run after the
shorter `format!` strings landed; folding them in keeps `cargo fmt
--check` clean for the next agent and prevents the formatter from
fighting with substantive edits in subsequent commits to the same
files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
